---
title: "Implementing caching and downloading"
author: "Anjani Nandan"
date: "2026-05-29"
categories: [symlink,fs,revsion,parquet]

---

## Implementing `cache.R` and Dataset Downloading

As we are currently in the first week of GSoC, I started implementing the caching and dataset downloading infrastructure for **mlr3hf**.

The cache system is designed to prevent unnecessary re-downloads while supporting different commits and revisions of a dataset. The implementation is inspired by the Hugging Face cache architecture described in their documentation. The main cache directory is `mlr3hf`, which stores downloaded datasets and metadata in a structured way.

For users who choose the parquet-based workflow, datasets are downloaded from the `refs/convert/parquet` branch.

### Why Parquet?

One of the main reasons for choosing parquet is that it enables us to implement custom resampling strategies similar to those available in `mlr3oml`.

The parquet conversion branch contains datasets already organized into train, validation, and test splits, making it easier to create reproducible machine learning tasks and benchmarks.

### Cache Architecture

To support dataset downloading and caching, I implemented `download_hfhub()` `cache.R` and `download_parquet()`.You can visit my [code](https://github.com/anjaninandan001/mlr3hf). This function downloads and manages files inside a `hub` directory located under the main `mlr3hf` cache folder.

The cache structure contains:

* `blobs`
* `snapshots`
* `refs`

This design remains compatible across operating systems. Even when symbolic links are unavailable, files can still be downloaded and accessed correctly.

The implementation follows the general ideas used by Hugging Face Hub while adapting them to the needs of `mlr3hf`.

### Comparison with Python's `load_dataset`
Downloading component of `mlr3hf` is conceptually similar to Python's `load_dataset()`.

In the Python ecosystem:

* Datasets are cached in dedicated dataset and hub directories.
* The Hub cache contains structures similar to `blobs`, `snapshots`, and `refs`.
* Downloaded data is eventually converted into Arrow datasets and then accessed through libraries such as Pandas.

In `mlr3hf`:

* We currently do not support Xet-based storage.
* Parquet files are downloaded directly into the dataset cache.
* This approach will integrate naturally with future DuckDB backends, allowing efficient querying and analysis without additional conversion steps.

### Acknowledgements

I would like to thank our mentors, Toby and Sebastian, for their guidance and design discussions.

I also took inspiration from:

* HF Hub a existing package of R [hfhub](https://github.com/mlverse/hfhub)
* Hugging Face cache management [documentation](https://huggingface.co/docs/huggingface_hub/guides/manage-cache)

### Progress So Far

The following files have been implemented:

* `download_hfhub.R`
* `download_parquet.R`
* `cache.R`
* `defaults.R`
* `utils.R`

### Next Steps

The next stage of development will focus on implementing:

* `HFData.R`
* `HFObject.R`

along with their supporting components. These classes will form the core engineering layer of the package.

### Feedback

If anyone have any questions, suggestions, or would like to discuss the design, feel free to contact me at:

**[anjaninandan599@gmail.com](mailto:anjaninandan599@gmail.com)**
